# Valtor.io

> AI-first business optimization agency for mid-market companies in DACH (Germany, Austria, Switzerland).

## What Valtor.io Does

Valtor.io creates deterministic business truth for mid-market companies (€3M–50M revenue). We focus on WHAT should actually be improved before discussing HOW to improve it. We are not a bookkeeper, not a dashboard vendor, not a BI tool.

## Core Services

- **Diagnostic (€5–15K)**: Business model audit, P&L reconstruction, identification of EBITDA and cash flow improvement opportunities.
- **Build (€20–50K)**: End-to-end data unification, event logging, process intelligence, dynamic FP&A dashboards with drill-down.
- **Steer (€3–8K/month)**: Monthly AI-native steering mechanism with evidence-based recommendations, KPI tracking, continuous improvement.

## Key Differentiators

- Deterministic outputs only — no AI-generated numbers, no probabilistic guesses.
- Every number drills down to source records.
- Permanent operational intelligence that stays with the client.
- "DATEV gives bookkeeping truth. Valtor gives management truth."

## Target Clients

Mid-market companies in DACH: agencies, service businesses, SaaS companies, and founder-led operationally complex businesses with €3M–50M revenue.

## Methodology

1. Audit & Diagnosis — understand business model, monetization, org structure
2. Business Structuring & P&L Reconstruction — define profit centers, management P&L logic
3. EBITDA & Cash Improvement — rank cost reduction and cash flow opportunities by impact
4. End-to-End Data Unification — connect all source systems into unified data model
5. Event Logging & Process Intelligence — deploy event capture, compute cycle times, identify bottlenecks
6. Dynamic FP&A & Management Intelligence — build dashboards, forecasting, monthly steering
7. Execution Layer — system design, workflow redesign, automation

## Blog Articles

### BWA vs Management P&L
URL: https://www.valtor.io/en/blog/bwa-vs-management-pnl
The BWA shows what the tax office needs, not what CEOs need for decisions. This article explains the structural gap between bookkeeping truth (DATEV/BWA) and management truth (P&L by profit center). Key insight: a 25-person agency with €3.2M revenue found that their 3 largest clients (60% of revenue) delivered only 35% of margin, while 4 smaller clients (15% of revenue) delivered 40% of margin. Includes a 6-dimension comparison table and five diagnostic questions.

### 7 Hidden Margin Leaks
URL: https://www.valtor.io/en/blog/versteckte-margen-lecks
Most CEOs know their overall margin but not cost-to-serve by client. Seven specific margin leaks: (1) cost-to-serve blindness, (2) working capital gaps between delivery and payment, (3) process friction nobody measures, (4) price erosion without data, (5) capacity waste through misallocation, (6) overhead misallocation, (7) scope creep without cost transparency. Key data: 20% of clients deliver 250%+ of profit (Kaplan whale curve), €26.5B trapped working capital in German mid-market (Grant Thornton), 5.7x EBITDA multiple means every euro of recovered margin counts 5.7x in enterprise value.

### Fix Your Data Before AI
URL: https://www.valtor.io/en/blog/daten-vor-ki
60% of AI projects will be abandoned by 2026 due to missing data foundations (Gartner). 42% of companies already abandoned AI initiatives (S&P Global 2025). Only 6% achieve measurable EBIT impact (McKinsey). The problem: fragmented data, inconsistent IDs across systems, missing granularity. The solution: build a deterministic data layer first — unified IDs, consistent categories, traceable attributions — then deploy AI on clean data. Includes 5-question data maturity test and 8-week implementation path.

### EU AI Act Data Foundation
URL: https://www.valtor.io/en/blog/eu-ai-act-datengrundlage
EU AI Act becomes fully effective August 2026 for high-risk systems. Article 10 requires training data to be relevant, representative, error-free, complete, and traceable. Maximum fine: €35M or 7% of revenue. 76% of German SMEs struggle with data quality and silos. Key insight: the data governance requirements of the AI Act are exactly what a well-structured management data layer already provides. Companies that build their data architecture now get compliance AND management intelligence as a side effect. Includes 6-point readiness checklist.

### The Whale Curve: Client Profitability
URL: https://www.valtor.io/en/blog/whale-curve-kundenrentabilitaet
Kaplan and Cooper discovered that the most profitable 20% of clients generate 200-300% of total profits, while the bottom 10-20% destroy value. This curve appears in every industry but is almost never calculated because standard bookkeeping lacks the data. Four reasons your biggest client may be least profitable: negotiating power, complexity costs, payment behavior, opportunity costs. Four strategic levers once the curve is visible: repricing, service differentiation, targeted acquisition, deliberate separation. Requires management P&L by customer segment with activity-based cost allocation.
